Changelog entries display - Version grouping works - Date formatting correct - Links to related content 2. **What's New Modal** - Shows for new features - Dismisses correctly - Don't show again works - Images/GIFs display 3. **Feature Announcements** - Banner for major features - Targetted to relevant users - Dismiss persists - Links work 4. **RSS/Atom Feed** - Feed URL accessible - Feed content valid - New entries appear ## Logging Requirements - Log changelog views - Log what's new interactions - Log banner dismissals - Screenshot modal states ## Files to Create - e2e/changelog/page.spec.ts - e2e/changelog/whats-new.spec.ts - e2e/changelog/announcements.spec.ts ## Success Criteria - Changelog displays correctly - What's new helpful - Users stay informed - Not intrusive ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:22:28.449971001-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:22:28.449971001-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-38vm","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ezv2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:34.506342812-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3968","title":"Task: E2E test - Mobile viewport interactions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:48:17.043234743-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:16:59.548383147-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:16:59.548383147-05:00","close_reason":"Covered in homepage.spec.ts (Responsive Layout tests) and prompt-detail.spec.ts (Mobile Bottom Sheet tests) - tests mobile viewport shows correct layout, touch-friendly buttons, and bottom sheet behavior on 390x844 viewport with hasTouch enabled","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3968","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-945","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:58:11.341337517-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3b05","title":"Task: Create TestLogger class with levels (debug/info/step/error)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:46:26.173743647-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T18:22:20.5322002-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T18:22:20.5322002-05:00","close_reason":"Created TestLogger class with debug/info/step/error levels, timestamps, context formatting, and file output for CI artifacts","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3b05","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-50cn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:54:52.182623225-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3fms","title":"Add visual separation between Featured and Browse sections","description":"# Task: Clarify Featured vs Browse Section Boundary ## Problem Statement Currently, the Featured section appears immediately after the Hero, followed by the filter controls and browse grid. This creates confusion because: 1. **Filters don't affect featured**: User applies a filter, sees 6 featured prompts unchanged, thinks filtering is broken 2. **No visual boundary**: Featured and Browse blend together 3. **Duplicated prompts**: Featured prompts may appear again in the browse grid ## Current Layout ``` Hero (search + category pills) ↓ Featured Prompts (6 cards, unaffected by filters) ↓ Filter Controls (Category, Tags, Clear) ↓ Results Header ("All Prompts" / "Search Results") ↓ Browse Grid (filtered results) ``` ## Proposed Solution ### Option A: Move Featured Above Filters, Add Clear Separator ```tsx {/* Hero */} {/* Featured Section - clearly labeled, visually distinct */}

Featured Prompts

Curated by the team
{/* Clear visual break */}
{/* Browse Section - with filters */}

Browse All Prompts

``` ### Option B: Integrate Featured into Results Remove separate featured section. Instead: - Add "Featured" badge to featured prompts in the grid - Sort featured prompts to top by default - Filters affect all prompts including featured ```tsx // In PromptGrid or page.tsx const sortedPrompts = useMemo(() => { // Featured first, then the rest const featured = filteredPrompts.filter(p => p.featured); const nonFeatured = filteredPrompts.filter(p => !p.featured); return [...featured, ...nonFeatured]; }, [filteredPrompts]); ``` ### Option C: Collapsible Featured Section (Best of Both) Show featured by default, allow collapsing: ```tsx const [showFeatured, setShowFeatured] = useState(true);
Featured Prompts {featuredCount}
``` ## Recommended Approach **Option A** is simplest and clearest: - Distinct visual sections with clear labels - Gradient separator creates elegant break - "Curated by the team" explains why they're special - Users understand filters apply only to "Browse" section ## Implementation Details 1. Add section wrapper with border-bottom to FeaturedPromptsSection 2. Add "Browse All Prompts" heading above filter controls 3. Add subtle gradient divider between sections 4. Consider adding "(not affected by filters)" note near Featured heading ## Testing 1. Apply category filter - featured section unchanged, browse section filtered 2. Verify visual separation is clear in both light/dark modes 3. Screen reader: sections are announced as distinct regions 4. Mobile: sections stack correctly ## Files to Modify - apps/web/src/app/page.tsx - apps/web/src/components/landing/FeaturedPromptsSection.tsx (possibly) ## Dependencies - Should be done after color consistency (page.tsx) - Affects layout, may need responsive testing","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:47:27.281567949-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T00:47:27.281567949-05:00"} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3fms","title":"Add visual separation between Featured and Browse sections","description":"# Task: Clarify Featured vs Browse Section Boundary ## Problem Statement Currently, the Featured section appears immediately after the Hero, followed by the filter controls and browse grid. This creates confusion because: 1. **Filters don't affect featured**: User applies a filter, sees 6 featured prompts unchanged, thinks filtering is broken 2. **No visual boundary**: Featured and Browse blend together 3. **Duplicated prompts**: Featured prompts may appear again in the browse grid ## Current Layout ``` Hero (search + category pills) ↓ Featured Prompts (6 cards, unaffected by filters) ↓ Filter Controls (Category, Tags, Clear) ↓ Results Header ("All Prompts" / "Search Results") ↓ Browse Grid (filtered results) ``` ## Proposed Solution ### Option A: Move Featured Above Filters, Add Clear Separator ```tsx {/* Hero */} {/* Featured Section - clearly labeled, visually distinct */}

Featured Prompts

Curated by the team
{/* Clear visual break */}
{/* Browse Section - with filters */}

Browse All Prompts

``` ### Option B: Integrate Featured into Results Remove separate featured section. Instead: - Add "Featured" badge to featured prompts in the grid - Sort featured prompts to top by default - Filters affect all prompts including featured ```tsx // In PromptGrid or page.tsx const sortedPrompts = useMemo(() => { // Featured first, then the rest const featured = filteredPrompts.filter(p => p.featured); const nonFeatured = filteredPrompts.filter(p => !p.featured); return [...featured, ...nonFeatured]; }, [filteredPrompts]); ``` ### Option C: Collapsible Featured Section (Best of Both) Show featured by default, allow collapsing: ```tsx const [showFeatured, setShowFeatured] = useState(true);
Featured Prompts {featuredCount}
``` ## Recommended Approach **Option A** is simplest and clearest: - Distinct visual sections with clear labels - Gradient separator creates elegant break - "Curated by the team" explains why they're special - Users understand filters apply only to "Browse" section ## Implementation Details 1. Add section wrapper with border-bottom to FeaturedPromptsSection 2. Add "Browse All Prompts" heading above filter controls 3. Add subtle gradient divider between sections 4. Consider adding "(not affected by filters)" note near Featured heading ## Testing 1. Apply category filter - featured section unchanged, browse section filtered 2. Verify visual separation is clear in both light/dark modes 3. Screen reader: sections are announced as distinct regions 4. Mobile: sections stack correctly ## Files to Modify - apps/web/src/app/page.tsx - apps/web/src/components/landing/FeaturedPromptsSection.tsx (possibly) ## Dependencies - Should be done after color consistency (page.tsx) - Affects layout, may need responsive testing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:47:27.281567949-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T02:46:09.758005579-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T02:46:09.758005579-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Option A: Added 'Featured Prompts' title with 'Curated by the team' subtitle, border-bottom on Featured section, gradient divider between sections, 'Browse All Prompts' section heading, and contextual results count. Build passes."} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3jiy","title":"Implement moderation queue prioritization","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-0om4. ## Task Prioritize moderation queue based on urgency and impact. ## Priority Factors - Report count (more reports = higher priority) - Reporter reputation (trusted reporters boost priority) - Content author tier (premium users slightly lower priority for false reports) - Report reason severity (illegal > harassment > spam) - Time in queue (aging boost) ## Features - Auto-calculated priority score - Priority badges in queue - Filter by priority level - SLA tracking (time to review) - Auto-escalation for stale items ## Database Changes - Add priority_score to reported_content - Add escalated_at, reviewed_by - Create priority calculation function ## UI - Priority column with color coding - Sort by priority (default) - Priority breakdown on hover - SLA countdown indicator ## Success Criteria - Critical items reviewed first - Fair and consistent scoring - SLA compliance tracking - Escalation notifications ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:17:02.161025256-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:17:02.161025256-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3jiy","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fhci","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:29.400235563-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3jiy","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-kk7l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:29.527931921-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3kx","title":"Implement jfp suggest command","description":"Part 14.2 of PLAN. 'jfp suggest ' suggests relevant prompts for a task. Uses BM25 search by default. Supports --semantic flag for optional semantic rerank. --json output for agents. Shows prompt relevance scores and brief descriptions. Helps users discover prompts for their current task.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:46:32.384175484-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:12:15.044824264-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:12:15.044824264-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented jfp suggest with BM25 search, relevance bars, and contextual tips","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3kx","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-z16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:49:17.014042309-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3kx","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xdt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:49:17.040792653-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3plh","title":"Task: CategoryFilter component unit tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"agent-claude","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:44:52.601116655-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:38:29.805660659-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:38:29.805660659-05:00","close_reason":"Created 16 unit tests for CategoryFilter component covering rendering, interactions, accessibility, and edge cases.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-3plh","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wxw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:55:12.299518354-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
